## Estimating Total Giraffe Spots Worldwide

### Step 1: Current Giraffe Population
- Wild giraffes (IUCN 2022 estimates): ~117,000
- Captive giraffes (zoos, reserves): ~1,600
- **Total: ~119,000 giraffes**

Giraffes have distinct polygonal patches (technically called "blotches") separated by lighter lines. These vary by taxonomy:

| Type | Approx. patch count | Approx. population |
|------|--------------------|--------------------|
| Reticulated giraffe | ~350 (large, well-defined) | ~16,000 |
| Masai giraffe | ~280 (irregular, jagged) | ~45,000 |
| Southern giraffe | ~300 (rounded) | ~44,000 |
| Northern giraffe (all ssp.) | ~270 (lighter, fewer) | ~5,900 |
| Captive (mixed) | ~300 | ~1,600 |

Weighted average patches per giraffe: **~300**

### Step 3: Calculation

$$119{,}000 \text{ giraffes} \times 300 \text{ patches/giraffe} \approx 35{,}700{,}000$$

---

## **Final Estimate: 36,000,000**

### Step 1: Total Giraffe Population

Based on Giraffe Conservation Foundation data (~2022):

| Northern | Irregular, variable | ~250 |

Weighted average across species: **~265 patches per giraffe**

$$118{,}000 \text{ giraffes} \times 265 \text{ patches} \approx 31{,}270{,}000$$
